name: SmartBooks
description_short: SmartBooks provides outsourced bookkeeping, accounting, tax, and fractional CFO services for small businesses and professional service firms since 2009.
description_long:
SmartBooks was founded in 2009 by a small business entrepreneur with the mission to help business owners focus on growth while their back-office financial operations are handled by experts. The company has worked with hundreds of small businesses across diverse industries and positions itself as a comprehensive financial partner rather than a single-service provider.

SmartBooks offers a tiered service menu including bookkeeping and accrual accounting, fractional CFO advisory services, tax planning and preparation, and implementation of The Financial Operating System®—a proprietary framework for financial management. They also provide QuickBooks expert assessments, financial performance metrics analysis, and specialized support for specific industries including marketing/advertising agencies, professional services firms, software/technology companies, and youth sports programs. Current promotional offerings include discounted QuickBooks training and 50% off first-month bookkeeping services.

The company differentiates itself through industry-specific expertise, a founder-led approach to client relationships, and the exclusive licensing of The Financial Operating System® methodology. They emphasize scalability for growing companies, offering support tailored to business lifecycle stages—launching, scaling, and maturing. Their service model allows clients to select packages based on current needs rather than forcing all-in solutions.

SmartBooks operates as a service-based accounting firm rather than a lender or financial product provider. While the "business-loans" category is technically correct per the available taxonomy, this is fundamentally a back-office outsourcing and advisory firm, not a financing company. Prospective clients should understand they are purchasing professional services (with associated costs), not accessing credit or capital.
pros: Founded by a small business entrepreneur with 15+ years of industry experience and demonstrable understanding of owner pain points
Offers industry-specific expertise for marketing/advertising, professional services, software/technology, and youth sports organizations
Licensed provider of The Financial Operating System®, a proprietary financial management framework designed specifically for small business owners
Fractional CFO services provide C-level financial guidance without the cost of a full-time executive
Flexible service packages allow businesses to select only needed services rather than mandatory bundled offerings
Free initial consultation available to assess business needs and recommend appropriate service tier
Promotional pricing available including $250 discounted assessments and 50% off first month of bookkeeping
cons: Pricing structure not transparently disclosed on website—prospective clients must schedule a consultation to learn actual costs
Service-based model means ongoing monthly expenses rather than one-time financing or capital access
No indication of technology integration capabilities beyond QuickBooks support—unclear how they handle other accounting software
Limited information about turnaround times, response times, or service level agreements for bookkeeping and tax services
Testimonials are minimal and incomplete (one quote is cut off mid-sentence), providing limited third-party validation
best_for: Small business owners spending excessive time on accounting who want to redirect focus to core operations
Marketing agencies, professional services firms, and software companies needing industry-specific financial guidance
Growing companies seeking scalable financial systems and fractional CFO support without full executive hire costs
Business owners preparing for exit or sale who need polished financial reporting and analysis
diagnosis: SmartBooks is ideal for small business owners and professional service firm leaders who need expert back-office accounting and fractional financial leadership but lack the budget for full-time CFO hires. The primary caveat is that this is a service expense, not a financing product—clients pay ongoing fees for outsourced expertise rather than accessing capital or credit.
services: Fractional CFO services and business advisory
The Financial Operating System® implementation and training
QuickBooks expert assessment and Q&A training
Financial performance metrics analysis and consultation
Industry-specific accounting setup (marketing, professional services, technology, youth sports)
Scalable financial procedures and policies for growing companies
SportsEngine integration and financial reporting for youth sports programs
